Index.title: ENISo ACM Student Chapter
Index.description: The official chartered ACM student chapter at ENISo.
Index.pitch_text: We are more than just a club. We are an official part of the global ACM network, dedicated to advancing computing as a science and profession. Our mission is to provide students with the resources, mentorship, and opportunities needed to excel in the tech industry.
Index.why_acm_global_text: The Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) is the world's largest educational and scientific computing society. With over 100,000 members across 190 countries, ACM advances computing as a science and profession, fostering innovation and leadership in every corner of the tech world.
Index.why_acm_chapter_text: At ENISo, we turn bright ideas into real code, real events, and real connections—powered by hack nights, mentors, and projects that actually ship.
Index.why_acm_chapter_followup: Hit ACM’s chapter directory, jump in, and join the crew that’s charging the future of computing.
Index.faq_subtitle: Everything you need to know about our chapter.

About.hero_intro: Our chapter is built around five core pillars that define our mission and shape everything we do. These pillars guide our events, initiatives, and opportunities for members.

community.title: Community
community.text: At the heart of our chapter is a vibrant community of students passionate about computing and technology. We aim to create a welcoming environment where members can meet like-minded peers, collaborate on ideas, and grow together. Through social events, team projects, and collaborative learning, we foster a culture where curiosity, creativity, and mutual support thrive.

technical.title: Technical Skills
technical.text: We believe the best way to learn technology is by building and doing. Our chapter organizes workshops, coding sessions, and hands-on technical events that help members develop practical skills beyond the classroom. Members gain experience with modern tools, programming languages, and software development practices within the broader field of Computer Science.

industry.title: Industry Connections
industry.text: Preparing students for real-world careers is a key part of our mission. We build bridges between students and the technology industry by hosting guest speakers, networking events, and company collaborations. Through these initiatives, members gain valuable insight into professional pathways and opportunities within the global computing community supported by the Association for Computing Machinery.

academic.title: Research & Academic Growth
academic.text: For members interested in deeper academic exploration, our chapter promotes research engagement and intellectual curiosity. We organize research talks, academic workshops, and mentorship opportunities that introduce students to the world of scientific discovery and innovation. Our goal is to help students develop the skills needed for graduate studies, research internships, and academic contributions.

competitive.title: Competitive Programming
competitive.text: We foster a strong culture of algorithmic thinking and problem-solving through structured training sessions, internal contests, and collaborative practice. Our members regularly work on data structures, algorithms, and contest strategies to strengthen their performance under timed conditions. We actively prepare and support teams to participate in major competitions.

Team.hero_subtitle: Building trust, showcasing leadership, and recognizing contributors.
Team.alumni_subtitle: A legacy section honoring past leaders and showing where they work now.

roles.chair: The highest leadership position of the chapter. Sets the overall vision, direction, and culture of the club. Presides over all meetings, makes final decisions, represents the chapter at university and ACM national levels, and ensures all committees are functioning effectively. Accountable for the chapter's success as a whole.
roles.vice_chair: Supports the Chair in all duties and steps in as acting Chair when needed. Oversees inter-committee coordination, ensures directors are meeting their goals, and acts as the internal operations manager. Often the person officers go to for day-to-day guidance.
roles.treasurer: Manages all financial matters of the chapter. Responsible for budgeting, tracking expenditures, collecting dues, processing reimbursements, applying for university funding, and maintaining transparent financial records. Coordinates with directors on event budgets and reports finances to the executive board regularly.
roles.membership_chair: Owns the full member lifecycle — from recruitment and onboarding to engagement and retention. Tracks membership rosters, manages dues status, coordinates welcome events, and ensures new members feel integrated into the chapter community.
roles.secretary: The organizational backbone of the executive board. Records and distributes meeting minutes, maintains official chapter documents, manages internal communications and announcements, coordinates scheduling, and ensures the chapter meets ACM national reporting requirements.
roles.faculty_sponsor: A faculty or staff member who serves as the chapter's institutional anchor. Provides academic guidance, signs off on official university and ACM paperwork, lends credibility to the chapter, connects students with research and professional opportunities, and advises leadership on long-term strategy.
roles.events_director: Oversees the planning, coordination, and execution of all chapter events. Manages the semester event calendar, coordinates logistics like venues, catering, and equipment, and ensures events run smoothly from conception to wrap-up. Works closely with Marketing and Technical directors for promotion and support.
roles.marketing_director: Responsible for the chapter's brand, visibility, and communications. Oversees all promotional materials, social media presence, and public-facing messaging. Ensures consistent branding across all channels and manages the chapter's reputation both on campus and externally.
roles.technical_director: Leads all technical initiatives within the chapter. Oversees the chapter website, internal tools, member projects, and technical workshops. Fosters a culture of hands-on learning and ensures members have opportunities to build real technical skills.
roles.research_director: Bridges the gap between the chapter and academic life. Organizes research talks, academic workshops, study resources, and faculty connections. Supports members interested in graduate school, research internships, and academic publishing.
roles.industry_director: Manages all relationships between the chapter and the professional world. Reaches out to companies for sponsorships, coordinates corporate speakers and recruiters, and secures funding and resources from industry partners. Maintains a pipeline of company contacts and tracks sponsorship agreements.
roles.cp_director: Leads all competitive programming and technical contest efforts. Organizes practice sessions, coaches members for competitions like ICPC, and fosters a culture of algorithmic thinking and problem-solving excellence within the chapter.

Join.hero_subtitle: Convert your passion into official ACM membership.
Join.steps_intro: Follow these steps to become a member of our chapter. Most steps take just a few minutes, and everything is designed to help you get started quickly.

application.title: Complete the chapter registration form
application.desc: Fill out the official ACM chapter membership form to start your application.

interview.title: Attend a short interview with our team
interview.desc: Chat with our team so we can learn about your interests and match you with opportunities.

payment.title: Pay the required membership fee
payment.desc: Submit the required fee (if applicable) to confirm your spot in the chapter.

confirmation.title: Receive confirmation and officially join the chapter
confirmation.desc: Once approved, you’ll get a welcome message with next steps and how to join our community.

Join.membership_open_note: Our membership form is open throughout the academic year, except during the summer break. We encourage interested students to apply when registrations are available.
Join.cta_text: When you’re ready, click the button below to open the official membership form. We review submissions regularly and will follow up soon.

Contact.hero_subtitle: Accessibility for sponsors, speakers, and students.
